description.value:
The Sleeping Goddess has blessed you with sprouts that are truly intoxicating.

Prerequisites: Two @UUID[Compendium.pf1-pow.disciplines.Item.iwfbEXyacCnVLEoH]{Intoxicating Petal} maneuvers, two @UUID[Compendium.pf1-pow.disciplines.Item.jXUBU7aO1TYYDTUk]{Sleeping Goddess} maneuvers

Benefit: The first time in a 24 hour period that a creature receives a sprout stack from you, they are befuddled. A befuddled creature treats no creatures as allies and all creatures as enemies, other than themself, for a number of rounds equal to 1/4 your initiator level (rounded down, minimum 1). The sprouted creature receives a saving throw (Will save DC 10 + 1/2 initiator level + initiation modifier) to negate this effect. Creatures treated as [sprout 0] by one of your abilities are not considered to have received a sprout stack for the purposes of this ability. Once a creature has been befuddled by you and recovered from the condition, they are immune to being befuddled by you for 24 hours.

When you hit a sprouted foe with a maneuver that was augmented by spending power points, they must succeed at another Will saving throw (as per above) or have the duration of their befuddled effect extended by the number of power points spent. Each time a creature fails a saving throw against this effect, they gain a stacking +2 bonus on saving throws against it until they succeed at the saving throw. If the sprouted foe was not befuddled at the time, they are instead forced to make a new saving throw to avoid becoming befuddled, as per above.
